procedure_description: Lumbar transforaminal epidural steroid injection, L4-L5 level, with fluoroscopic guidance

clinical_notes.chief_complaint: Left leg radiculopathy and low back pain, 8/10 severity
conservative_therapy: Naproxen 500mg twice daily for 8 weeks (failed — inadequate relief)

imaging.findings: L4-L5 left paracentral disc herniation with compression of L5 nerve root. Moderate foraminal stenosis at L4-L5.

clinical_notes.procedure_note: Informed consent obtained. Patient placed prone. L4-L5 transforaminal approach under live fluoroscopy. Contrast confirmed epidural spread. 40mg triamcinolone + 2mL 0.25% bupivacaine injected. Patient tolerated procedure well. No complications.

procedure_description: Lumbar interlaminar epidural steroid injection, L5-S1

clinical_notes.chief_complaint: Bilateral low back pain without radiation, 5/10 severity

imaging.findings: No acute bony abnormality. Mild disc space narrowing at L5-S1.

clinical_notes.procedure_note: Interlaminar ESI performed at L5-S1 without imaging guidance. 40mg methylprednisolone injected. No complications noted.

procedure_description: 4th lumbar epidural steroid injection, same region (L3-L5), this year
diagnosis_descriptions: Subluxation stenosis of neural canal of lumbar region

imaging.findings: Severe spinal canal stenosis at L3-L4 and L4-L5 with bilateral foraminal narrowing. Ligamentum flavum hypertrophy.

clinical_notes.procedure_note: 4th lumbar ESI this year requested. Fluoroscopy guidance used. L4-L5 interlaminar approach. 40mg triamcinolone injected. Patient has diabetes mellitus type 2 — glucose check performed pre-procedure (186 mg/dL). Endocrinologist notified.
